channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Anthropic] Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5
item.description: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5

item.title: [Hacker News] System Card: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 [pdf]
item.description: System Card: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 [pdf]

item.title: [Hacker News] Claude Fable 5
item.description: Claude Fable 5

item.title: [Hacker News] 'Sloppenheimer:' Amazon Employees Mock the Company's AI on Slack
item.description: 'Sloppenheimer:' Amazon Employees Mock the Company's AI on Slack

item.title: [Google DeepMind] Fluid, natural voice translation with Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
item.description: Fluid, natural voice translation with Gemini 3.5 Live Translate

item.title: [Google DeepMind] Introducing Gemma 4 12B: a unified, encoder-free multimodal model
item.description: Introducing Gemma 4 12B: a unified, encoder-free multimodal model

item.title: [Google DeepMind] Powering the future of robotics in Europe
item.description: Powering the future of robotics in Europe

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/x1xhlol — FULL Augment Code, Claude Code, Cluely, CodeBuddy, Comet, Cursor, Devin AI, Junie, Kiro, Leap.new, Lovable, Manus, NotionAI, Orchids.app, Perplexity, Poke, Qoder, Replit, Same.dev, Trae, Traycer AI, VSCode Agent, Warp.dev, Windsurf, Xcode, Z.ai Code, Dia & v0. (And other Open Sourced) System Prompts, Internal Tools & AI Models
item.description: Repository containing system prompts and tools from 25+ AI-powered coding assistants including Claude Code, Cursor, Devin AI, and Windsurf.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] maziyarpanahi/openmed — open-source healthcare ai
item.description: maziyarpanahi/openmed is an open-source healthcare AI project trending on GitHub.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] addyosmani/agent-skills — Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.
item.description: GitHub project provides production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.

item.title: [Hacker News] Cleaning up after AI rockstar developers
item.description: Cleaning up after AI rockstar developers

item.title: [Hacker News] Microsoft's open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers
item.description: Microsoft's open source tools were hacked to steal passwords of AI developers

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] FrontierCode: Benchmarking for Code Quality over Slop
item.description: [AINews] FrontierCode: Benchmarking for Code Quality over Slop

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Offline Reinforcement Learning for Plasma Control in Nuclear Fusion: Codebase and Benchmark
item.description: Offline reinforcement learning benchmark RL4F provides evaluation environments and baselines for plasma control in nuclear fusion using historical tokamak data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MedicalRec: Medical recommender system for image classification without retraining
item.description: MedicalRec recommends optimal medical image classification models using a dataset of 5,000+ model records without requiring retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SPIN: Decentralized Swarm Control via Tensorized Policy Coordination
item.description: SPIN framework enables decentralized multi-agent swarm control on edge devices by factorizing joint policies into tensor networks, reducing computational complexity from exponential to linear.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Boundary Variance Inflation Causes Acquisition Bias in Gaussian Processes
item.description: Kernel truncation at domain boundaries inflates Gaussian process variance, causing acquisition bias independent of task objectives.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Emergence via Phase Transitions: Mechanism Landscapes and Universal Convergence Across Complex Systems
item.description: Hierarchical Emergence Framework explains universal convergence of complex systems toward similar structures through phase transitions in mechanism landscapes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] STARIXNet: Multivariate and Multi-attribute Deep Learning Approach to Real-Time Resource Allocation in Cloud Platforms
item.description: STARIXNet uses multivariate metrics and spatio-temporal analysis for automated cloud resource allocation, demonstrating 10-50% cost savings at Walmart.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TriHead-GAN: A Generative Adversarial Network with Triple-Head Discriminator for Carbon Emission Time Series Generation
item.description: TriHead-GAN generates carbon emission time series data using a triple-head discriminator that supervises distributional authenticity, cross-variable correlations, and temporal smoothness.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Enabling KV Caching of Shared Prefix for Diffusion Language Models
item.description: Researchers propose bicache to enable KV caching for diffusion language models, achieving 36-98% throughput improvement over existing techniques.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] When Should an AI Scientist Stop? Verifiable Experiment Steering and Refusal for Autonomous Discovery
item.description: CARTOGRAPH guides autonomous AI systems to refuse inadequate experiments through experiment steering and structural model-fit detection.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MST-Direct at Scale: Multivariate and Conditional Geostatistical Simulation via Sinkhorn Optimal Transport
item.description: MST-Direct scales optimal transport for multivariate and conditional geostatistical simulation, exactly preserving joint distributions and honoring observed data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Training-Inference Kernel Contracts: Bounding Divergence in Post-Training and Deployment
item.description: Kernel contracts framework bounds divergence between training-optimized and inference-optimized kernels in post-training.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Customer Churn Prediction on Structured Data Using FT-Transformer and Stacking Ensembles
item.description: Hybrid model combining FT-Transformer and XGBoost via stacking improves customer churn prediction on structured data, achieving 62.10% F1.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Outage Detection in Self-Healing Smart Grids Using Reinforcement Learning with Spectral Graph Neural Networks
item.description: Researchers develop spectral graph neural networks with reinforcement learning to automatically optimize real-time power grid restoration during outages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] From Human Guidance to Autonomy: Agent Skill System for End-to-End LLM Deployment on Spatial NPUs
item.description: Agent skill system autonomously deploys eight LLM models on AMD XDNA 2 NPUs, achieving 2.2x to 4x performance improvements.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Routing Plateau: Understanding and Breaking the Accuracy Limits of LLM Routers
item.description: Study reveals LLM routing methods converge to a plateau caused by learning global trends instead of query-specific patterns; improvements need larger datasets and stronger encoders.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Optimality of Sequential Filtering Under Independent Cost and Selectivity Models
item.description: Researchers prove sequential filters should be ordered by cost-to-rejection-probability ratio to minimize total expected cost, outperforming heuristic approaches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ResearchClawBench: A Benchmark for End-to-End Autonomous Scientific Research
item.description: ResearchClawBench benchmark evaluates AI agents' autonomous scientific research capability across 40 tasks; current systems are far from reliable reproduction of published research.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] UNIQ: Conformal Calibration for Adaptive Conservatism in Offline Reinforcement Learning
item.description: UNIQ improves offline RL by using conformal uncertainty estimates to adaptively adjust conservatism across states, balancing performance and computational efficiency.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Shortcuts in the Tail: Debiasing via Post-Hoc Spectral Compression of Fine-Tuning Updates
item.description: Truncating the tail of fine-tuning weight SVD reduces group bias gaps without retraining or group labels.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Repetition Mismatch: Why Data Mixture Experiments Don't Scale and How to Fix Them
item.description: Repetition rate mismatches cause small-scale data mixture experiments to fail at larger scales; repetition-controlled subsampling recovers optimal mixtures efficiently with minimal token budget.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Topological Characterization of Graph Neural Networks via Stochastic Block Model Embeddings on the n-Sphere
item.description: Topological framework maps trained GNNs onto n-sphere to create low-dimensional fingerprints enabling model comparison and transfer-learning candidate retrieval.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] DiffoR: A Unified Continuous Generative Framework for Universal Ordinal Regression
item.description: DiffOR formulates ordinal regression as continuous generative learning using diffusion models instead of quantization-based classification.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reachability and asymptotics of Gaussian Transformer dynamics
item.description: Research proves Gaussian distributions remain exactly Gaussian through Transformers, reformulating layer dynamics as finite-dimensional control system connected to classical filtering theory.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LFNO: Bridging Laplace and Fourier via Transient-Steady Decomposition
item.description: LFNO decomposes dynamical system dynamics into transient and steady-state components using combined Laplace and Fourier neural operators.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Sample-Efficient Post-Training for LEGO Spatial-Physics Reasoning
item.description: Researchers propose PVPO, a sample-efficient reinforcement learning method improving LEGO assembly generation by coupling physical feasibility with geometric and semantic alignment.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MetaEvo: A Meta-Optimization Framework for Experience-Driven Agent Evolution
item.description: MetaEvo enables LLM agents to continually improve reasoning through preference-based optimization and principle reuse in modular architectures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Contribution Weights: A Geometrical Analysis of Self-Attention Transformers
item.description: Contribution Weights metric outperforms attention weights by incorporating value magnitude and directional alignment to measure transformer token importance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SRT: Super-Resolution for Time Series via Disentangled Rectified Flow
item.description: Novel SRT framework reconstructs high-resolution time series from low-resolution data via disentangled rectified flow and attention.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] QDSP: An Interpretable Structured Learning Framework for Predicting Death or Cerebral Palsy in Very Low Birth Weight Infants
item.description: QDSP, an interpretable machine learning framework, predicts mortality and cerebral palsy risk in very low birth weight infants with high accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Position: Genomic Model Research Must Move Beyond Anecdotal Evaluation of Interpretability Methods
item.description: Genomic ML interpretability methods often yield contradictory explanations; researchers propose systematic evaluation framework to replace anecdotal validation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Bidirectional Small-Granularity Search between Code and Text
item.description: Bidirectional code-text search task links code snippets with scientific descriptions using shared-encoder architecture, trained on automatically-generated and manually-annotated data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] TinyJudge: Unverifiable Constraint Alignment via Lightweight Specialist Ensembles
item.description: TinyJudge employs tiny language model ensembles to evaluate instruction-following constraints, achieving 10% performance gains and 3x faster training than LLM-as-judge baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Evaluating Hallucinations in Domain-Adapted Large Language Models
item.description: Fine-tuned Llama-2 shows improved performance on training-similar tasks but struggles with novel domain-specific reasoning, prone to hallucinations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Community-Specific Slang and Entity Detection via Semantic Shift in Fine-Tuned Language Models
item.description: Method identifies community-specific slang and unique entities using semantic shift in embeddings from fine-tuned language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Retrieval Augmented Generation Framework for the Nepali Legal Domain Question Answering
item.description: Researchers apply Retrieval Augmented Generation to Nepali legal question answering using case law data, achieving 91% precision and demonstrating RAG effectiveness for low-resource languages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ABLE: Representing and Mapping LLMs via Attribution-Based Large-model Embedding
item.description: ABLE uses tokenizer-agnostic attribution-based embeddings to efficiently represent and compare heterogeneous LLMs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Implicit Causal Graph Construction in Text via Chain Discovery
item.description: LLMs infer hidden causal chains connecting explicit cause-effect pairs in text to construct implicit causal graphs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] GraphLoRA: Structure-Aware Low-Rank Adaptation for Large Language Model Recommendation
item.description: GraphLoRA integrates graph message-passing into low-rank adaptation for improved LLM-based recommendation systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Post-training is (Massive) Supervised Learning
item.description: Researchers argue LLM post-training primarily functions as supervised distribution-fitting for specific benchmarks rather than developing genuine general capabilities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] BEACON: Behavioral Entropy Aggregation for Cross-Model Hallucination Detection in Large Language Models
item.description: BEACON is a black-box framework for detecting LLM hallucinations using multi-dimensional signals from model outputs, achieving 0.8123 AUROC and outperforming existing baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] CAPruner: Conceptual-Adjacent Scene Graph Pruner for Enhancing 3D Spatial Reasoning of Large Language Models
item.description: CAPruner prunes scene graphs for 3D vision-language tasks by combining semantic relevance and spatial proximity to preserve task-critical relations while reducing computational costs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Finding New Connections between Concepts from Medline Database Incorporating Domain Knowledge
item.description: Researchers develop a Literature-Based Discovery model to identify hidden connections between medical concepts through intermediate topics.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] mllm-shap: A Shapley Value Explainability Platform for Text-Audio Multimodal Large Language Models
item.description: mllm-shap introduces Shapley Value explainability for text-audio multimodal language models, addressing interleaved processing and computational efficiency challenges.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Principled Agent Debate: Adversarial Arbitration for Sycophancy Reduction in Large Language Models
item.description: Principled Agent Debate reduces sycophancy bias in RLHF models by arbitrating between opposing-disposition agents.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Bridging Traditional Explainability Methods and Multimodal Multilingual Models: An XAI-Based Analysis
item.description: Researchers extend Shapley Values to multimodal language models to explain how text and audio features influence model decisions using efficient computation strategies and phonetic alignment.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Multilingual Refusal Alignment for Safer Large Language Models
item.description: Multilingual training improves LLM safety across languages better than English-only alignment, using a new 12-language European dataset.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] From Architecture to Output: Structural Origins of Hallucination in Large Language Models and the Amplifying Role of Data
item.description: Study identifies three architectural mechanisms in LLMs—self-attention, maximum likelihood training, and autoregressive decoding—that structurally cause hallucinations amplified by dataset pathologies.
